scheduled_after.note: Predecessor locked Option B (44 long-tail P1s accepted as fix-as-you-touch). This follow-up tokenizes ONLY frequency ≥ 2 magic numbers per the same rationale — the rule that locked Option B was 'high-frequency tokens only'. Singletons stay deferred.

Substitute 7 occurrences of frame(6) across StatusDropdown / SyncStatusIndicator / ReadinessCard etc.
Substitute 16 occurrences of the 8 two-instance magic dims
Address 4 DS-MAGIC-PADDING findings (9, 10, 13, 6) via AppSpacing token or per-instance inline justification
Run make ui-audit and verify P1 count reduced to ≤ 14
Run make tokens-check + xcodebuild build (sim) to verify no regressions
Open PR + close out state.json + case study

_meta.scope_decision: Option B respected per parent feature lock; ceiling 44 → ~14 (singletons stay as fix-as-you-touch)
success_metrics: 0 new visual regressions on touched views (operator spot-check at PR review)
kill_criteria: Visual regression on any critical user-path screen (Home/Training/Nutrition/Auth/Settings) caused by a token substitution → revert + investigate
